data.description:
## Why

Documentation has drifted behind a **completed** bash→Python migration. The README
still tells users to install a bash engine that was deleted; the event-schema doc's
event-type table omits three live event types; one source comment is actively
false. These are the artifacts a new engineer/agent reads first and is most
directly misled by.

> **Plan-review correction (do not skip):** an earlier draft also claimed
> `docs/event-schema.md` says `SCHEMA_VERSION` is `2` and that the table omits
> `TAG_DELTA`. **Both are FALSE** — verified directly: `event-schema.md:64` already
> says "(currently `3`)" and line 49 already carries a full `TAG_DELTA` row. There
> is **no** version drift and **no** TAG_DELTA omission to fix. The only genuine
> table gap is `WORKFLOW_RUN` / `WORKFLOW_STEP` / `COMMITS`.

## What

A single doc-truth PR that: (1) removes the deleted-bash-engine narrative from the
README, (2) adds the three missing event types to `docs/event-schema.md`'s table,
(3) fixes the one actively-false source comment, (4) adds the missing tool/subsystem
mentions, and (5) archives two completed/orphaned planning docs.

## Scope

- In scope: `README.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, `src/rebar/__init__.py` (comment
  only), `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/architecture.md` (one paragraph), and creating
  `docs/archive/` for two stale planning docs.
- Out of scope: code behavior; rewriting the (correct, living)
  `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md`; the `docs/concurrency.md` /
  `exit-codes.md` stale `.sh` *function-name* citations (low value — defer).

## Context (citations)

- **README bash narrative (false):** `README.md:24` ("The bash + Python engine is
  wrapped verbatim under `src/rebar/_engine/`"); `README.md:82` ("`git`, `bash`,
  `jq` — required"); `README.md:197-214` (the "bash dispatcher" packaging note).
  Verified false: **0** `.sh` files under `src/` (deleted in commit `e3507895`
  "E7-final — delete the bash engine"); `bash` never spawned; `jq` CLI never
  invoked (`_store/hlc.py:24`, `canonical.py:14` explicitly keep jq out);
  `docs/bash-migration.md:3` declares the migration COMPLETE. Only `git` (always)
  and `acli` (live Jira) are genuine prerequisites; locking is pure-Python
  `fcntl.flock` (`_store/lock.py:29`). **Note:** an installed venv may still
  contain `.sh` files under `site-packages/rebar/_engine/` — that is a stale
  *install* artifact, not the source tree; do not let it confuse the edit.
- **Missing event types (the ONLY event-schema fix):** the event-type table
  (`docs/event-schema.md:44-58`) omits `WORKFLOW_RUN`, `WORKFLOW_STEP`, `COMMITS`
  — all live with real processors (`reducer/_processors.py:374,395,415,447`,
  dispatched ≈615-621) and present in `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`
  (`reducer/_version.py:65-95`). `TAG_DELTA` is **already** in the table (line 49)
  and `SCHEMA_VERSION` is **already** documented as `3` (line 64) — leave both
  alone.
- **Actively-false source comment:** `src/rebar/__init__.py:559` — "next_batch is
  the lone exception: still the bash orchestrator." Contradicted by the function
  itself (`next_batch` at ≈760 calls `_reads.next_batch` in-process; its own
  docstring at ≈763 says "Tier C retired the bash orchestrator").
- **CLAUDE.md missing tools (completeness):** `mcp_server.py` registers
  `get_workflow_status` (≈503), `get_workflow_result` (≈514), `render_workflow`
  (≈527), `review_code` (≈557), `scan_spec` (≈580), `run_workflow` (≈788) — none
  appear in CLAUDE.md's tool lists.
- **architecture.md gap:** `src/rebar/_io/` (NDJSON import/export backing
  `rebar.export_tickets`/`import_tickets`, CLI `export`/`import`,
  `docs/import-export.md`) is absent from the component map.
- **Stale planning docs:** `docs/tier-e-execution-plan.md` (a completed "RESUME
  HERE → E7e" handoff; all items landed; zero inbound links) and
  `docs/2026-06-13-oss-comparison-session-log.md` (completed, orphaned session
  log). KEEP `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (it has a live inbound link
  from `docs/experiments/README.md:4` and is a forward-looking spec).

## Implementation plan

1. **README:** rewrite line 24 to describe the Python engine (no bash); change the
   prerequisites at line 82 to `git` (always) + `acli` (live Jira only) + the
   existing util-linux `flock` note; rewrite the packaging note (197-214) to keep
   the *correct* conclusion (engine ships unpacked as package data —
   `engine_dir()` asserts the Python reconciler is present) while removing the
   bash-dispatcher premise. Cross-check against `docs/bash-migration.md` for the
   accurate post-migration description.
2. **event-schema.md:** add only `WORKFLOW_RUN`, `WORKFLOW_STEP`, `COMMITS` to the
   event-type table (44-58) with their processors and a one-line "Written by" each,
   matching the existing column shape. Do NOT touch the `SCHEMA_VERSION` line (64,
   already `3`) or the `TAG_DELTA` row (49, already present).
3. **Source comment:** fix `src/rebar/__init__.py:559` to state next_batch runs
   in-process (or delete the stale parenthetical).
4. **CLAUDE.md:** add the 6 tools to the Reads / Writes / LLM-ops lists as
   appropriate (`get_workflow_status`/`get_workflow_result`/`render_workflow` →
   reads; `run_workflow` → writes; `review_code`/`scan_spec` → LLM-gated).
5. **architecture.md:** add a short `_io/` bullet to the component map.
6. **Archive:** create `docs/archive/`, `git mv` the two stale planning docs into
   it, and add a one-line README in `docs/archive/` explaining it holds completed
   historical plans (git preserves full history regardless).
7. Grep the repo for any links to the moved docs and update them (the two are
   orphaned, so expect none — confirm).

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** removes the most actively-misleading artifacts; cheap, no code risk.
- **Cost/risk:** near-zero. Archiving (vs deleting) is the conservative choice —
  git already preserves history, but a `docs/archive/` keeps the docs greppable
  without implying they are current. Defer the low-value `.sh` function-name
  citations in concurrency.md/exit-codes.md to S8 or a later touch.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README contains no claim that bash/jq are required or that a bash engine
  exists; prerequisites list only `git` (+ `acli` for live Jira, + the flock note).
- [ ] `docs/event-schema.md`'s event-type table includes WORKFLOW_RUN,
  WORKFLOW_STEP, COMMITS (TAG_DELTA + `SCHEMA_VERSION` 3 were already correct — untouched).
- [ ] `src/rebar/__init__.py:559` no longer claims next_batch uses the bash orchestrator.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md lists the 6 previously-missing MCP tools; architecture.md mentions `_io/`.
- [ ] `docs/tier-e-execution-plan.md` and `docs/2026-06-13-oss-comparison-session-log.md`
  are moved under `docs/archive/`; no dangling links remain.
- [ ] `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` is NOT archived (it is living).
</content>
data.title: Documentation-truth sweep: remove deleted-bash-engine narrative + fix drift
